title: Prime Field And Composite Modulus Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite field composite nonfield and bad inverse-candidate QF_BV/DRAT regression seeds
solver_reuse.pressure: Two fixed-width field arithmetic conflicts: a composite-modulus obstruction where no 3-bit residue inv < 6 satisfies (2*inv) mod 6 = 1, and a prime-field bad inverse candidate where exact replay computes 3*4 mod 7 = 5 while the false inverse claim requires 1.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional finite-field rows only when they add distinct fixed-width inverse, distributivity, or table solver pressure; keep field extensions, algebraic closure, and quantified field theory in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed prime-field operations as deterministic BV formulas or finite tables; the composite-modulus no-inverse and bad prime-field inverse-candidate rows now have checked QF_BV/DRAT artifacts.
Replay inverse-table witnesses through Axeyum model evaluation.
Keep the composite-modulus QF_BV row tied to a source artifact, checked DRAT regression, and tamper-rejection learner page before claiming proof-object coverage.
Continue emitting checked BV/CNF evidence for exhaustive no-counterexample and no-inverse rows.
Keep field extensions, algebraic closure, and quantification over all fields marked Lean-horizon.
